ogTitle: Cunyarie vs Currency Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cunyarie and Currency Creek.
heading: Cunyarie vs Currency Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cunyarie, South Australia and Currency Creek, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Currency Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Cunyarie (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cunyarie or Currency Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cunyarie has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Currency Creek scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cunyarie scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Currency Creek scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cunyarie or Currency Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Currency Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
